headline: How a Targeted Valve Recall and Watchman Questions at Boston Scientific (BSX) Have Changed Its Investment Story
summary: Earlier in June 2026, the US Food and Drug Administration posted a Class II, firm-initiated recall of 9,750 Boston Scientific Orca sterile, single-use air/water and suction valves (lot 38400303, expiring January 20, 2029) after higher-than-expected reports of suction buttons sticking during gastrointestinal endoscopy procedures. The recall, which is ongoing and affects nationwide US distribution, highlights how even accessory devices can create operational and regulatory pressures for a...

headline: Boston Scientific (BSX) Recalls Orca Valves Nationwide After Device Malfunction
summary: Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) has initiated a nationwide recall of its Orca Single Use Air/Water and Suction Valves. The recall is voluntary and follows identification of a device malfunction affecting procedures across the United States. The action reflects a material operational event for the company, with implications for patient safety oversight. Boston Scientific is a major medical device company, so a recall tied to endoscopy accessories like the Orca Single Use Air/Water and Suction...
